MGM RESORTS INTERNATIONAL REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2022 FINANCIAL AND OPERATING RESULTS

• Record Adjusted Property EBITDAR in 4Q and full year 2022 for Las Vegas Strip Resorts and Regional Operations

• Share buybacks of $2.

78a95ca9-18db-4539-8d12-5692270ca1fa.label: text_chunk
78a95ca9-18db-4539-8d12-5692270ca1fa.text:
8 billion in 2022 and $352 million in 4Q22

• New $2 billion share repurchase program authorized by the Board of Directors

• Closed on the sale of the operations of The Mirage for $1.1 billion in cash

• Awarded new 10-year gaming concession in Macau

Las Vegas, Nevada, February 8,

3d5d99aa-6bd9-4090-b5f1-55081c192e59.label: text_chunk
3d5d99aa-6bd9-4090-b5f1-55081c192e59.text:
2023 – MGM Resorts International (NYSE: MGM) (“MGM Resorts” or the “Company”) today reported financial results for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2022.

“We  achieved  our  fifth  consecutive  quarter  of  record-breaking  Las  Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Adjusted Property EBITDAR in the fourth quarter,” said Bill Hornbuckle, Chief Executive Officer and President of MGM Resorts.

253920b2-1c6b-4b61-8434-e1cdfb93e24a.label: text_chunk
253920b2-1c6b-4b61-8434-e1cdfb93e24a.text: “What we accomplished in 2022 is nothing short of remarkable, and is a testament to our strategic plan, scale, brand strength, talented team, loyalty program, and the diverse geographies and channels in which we operate.

f343373d-d5de-401b-a472-bf01e7b51aa2.label: text_chunk
f343373d-d5de-401b-a472-bf01e7b51aa2.text:
We believe that there is strong momentum in our business and our 2023 outlook  remains  bright,  driven  by  a  robust  events  calendar  domestically,  MGM  China’s  rapid  year-to- date return to profitability and BetMGM's ongoing improvement in 2023.”
“We achieved exceptional results in the fourth quarter and remain optimistic in our outlook for this year.

2c11311f-7b79-4db3-9c56-bf410b01ea49.label: text_chunk
2c11311f-7b79-4db3-9c56-bf410b01ea49.text: We are also focused on free cash flow and believe our company presents a compelling yield, especially when  taking  into  account  the  value  in  our  share  price  for  excess  cash,  our  ownership  value  of  MGM China and BetMGM,” said Jonathan Halkyard, Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer of MGM Resorts.

489d7caf-33e1-45f9-984c-f50414fc3c4f.label: text_chunk
489d7caf-33e1-45f9-984c-f50414fc3c4f.text: “Our share buyback program continues to return capital to shareholders as we have already repurchased 4 million shares for $164 million year-to-date, adding to the 76 million shares we repurchased in 2022 and totaling approximately  $4.7 billion  since 2021.

94216fcc-7816-4fc2-b21b-ffd4eccb81c9.label: text_chunk
94216fcc-7816-4fc2-b21b-ffd4eccb81c9.text:
Beyond this, our Board of Directors  authorized an additional $2 billion for share buybacks.”

MGM Resorts Share Repurchases

During  the  fourth  quarter  of  2022,  the  Company  repurchased  approximately  11  million  shares  of  its common  stock  at  an  average  price  of  $32.96  per  share  for  an  aggregate  amount  of  $352  million, pursuant  to  the  March  2022  repurchase  plan.

29623fab-d61f-4cae-8a0b-95d3cd26192e.label: text_chunk
29623fab-d61f-4cae-8a0b-95d3cd26192e.text: The  remaining  availability  under  the  March  2022 repurchase  plan  was  $475  million  as  of  December  31,  2022.  All  shares  repurchased  under  the Company’s program have been retired.On  February  8,  2023,  the  Company  announced  that  its  Board  of  Directors  had  authorized  a  new  $2.

414118ac-ded6-4d28-a376-ece1b7c17ab0.text: 0 billion  share  repurchase  plan.  Furthermore,  the  Company  announced  that  our  Board  of  Directors  had determined  to  suspend  our  ongoing  regular  dividends  in  order  to  focus  on  our  preferred  method  of returning  value  to  shareholders  through  our  share  repurchase  plan.

72dfd702-969c-4c2f-8f06-2f3f17425098.text: To  the  extent  the  Company determines  to  reinstate  the  dividend  in  the  future,  the  amount,  declaration  and  payment  of  any  future dividends will be subject  to  the discretion of the Board  of Directors who will evaluate the Company's dividend policy from time to time based on factors it deems relevant, and contractual limitations.

"Adjusted EPS" is diluted earnings or loss per share adjusted to exclude property transactions, net, preopening and start-up expenses,  gain  on  REIT  transactions,  net,  gain  on  consolidation  of  CityCenter,  net,  foreign  currency  gain/loss  related  to MGM China's U.S.

 dollar-denominated debt,

b2f4f799-692f-44c4-ab64-7bf9a2ef667b.text: net gain/loss related to equity investments for which the Company has elected the  fair  value  option  of  ASC  825  and  equity  investments  accounted  for  under  ASC  321  for  which  there  is  a  readily determinable fair value and net gain/loss related to the Company's debt securities, change in the fair value of foreign currency contracts,

37152c6f-67da-4a25-96f9-793a05c0b311.label: text_chunk
37152c6f-67da-4a25-96f9-793a05c0b311.text:
mark-to-market    adjustments    related    to    MGP's    unhedged    interest    rate    swaps,  mark-to-market  adjustments related to CityCenter's unhedged interest rate swaps recorded within non-operating items from unconsolidated affiliates, and gain  related  to  CityCenter's  sale  of Harmon land recorded within income from unconsolidated affiliates.

Adjusted  EPS  is  a  non-GAAP  measure  and  is

4b8e1c06-75e9-4416-aa24-7b1433bdf779.label: text_chunk
4b8e1c06-75e9-4416-aa24-7b1433bdf779.text: presented  solely  as  a  supplemental  disclosure  to  reported  GAAP  measures because  management  believes  this  measure  is  useful  in  providing  period-to-period  comparisons  of  the  results  of  the Company's  continuing  operations  to  assist  investors  in  reviewing  the  Company's  operating  performance  over  time.

25119c95-0e8a-4194-b020-9b5de4692a06.label: text_chunk
25119c95-0e8a-4194-b020-9b5de4692a06.text: Management  believes  that  while  certain  items  excluded  from Adjusted  EPS  may  be  recurring  in  nature  and  should  not  be disregarded  in  evaluating  the Company's  earnings  performance,

157499b5-75bd-449c-b1d1-e6d1e9434159.label: text_chunk
157499b5-75bd-449c-b1d1-e6d1e9434159.text:
it  is  useful  to  exclude  such  items  when  comparing  current performance  to  prior  periods  because  these  items  can  vary  significantly  depending  on  specific  underlying  transactions  or events.

 Also, management believes certain excluded items, and items further discussed in footnote 2 below, may not relate specifically  to  current  operating  trends  or  be  indicative  of  future  results.

b8c1edde-b032-49e0-a4f8-861cb4fb3c13.label: text_chunk
b8c1edde-b032-49e0-a4f8-861cb4fb3c13.text:
Adjusted  EPS  should  not  be  construed  as  an alternative to GAAP earnings per share as an indicator of the Company's performance.

 In addition, Adjusted EPS may not be defined in the same manner by all companies and, as a result, may not be comparable to similarly titled non-GAAP financial measures of other companies.

87c6f2ea-1515-4e3a-949e-e04c78da76bc.label: text_chunk
87c6f2ea-1515-4e3a-949e-e04c78da76bc.text:
A reconciliation of Adjusted EPS to diluted earnings per share can be found under "Adjusted Diluted Earnings Per Share" included in this release.2.

"Adjusted  EBITDAR"  is  earnings  before  interest  and  other  non-operating  income  (expense),  taxes,  depreciation  and amortization,  preopening  and  start-up  expenses,  property  transactions,  net,  gain  on  REIT  transactions,

8fbe1541-75a1-44da-bed0-0bd3fa162be0.label: text_chunk
8fbe1541-75a1-44da-bed0-0bd3fa162be0.text: net,  gain  on consolidation  of  CityCenter,  net,  rent  expense  related  to  triple-net  operating  leases  and  ground  leases,  gain  related  to CityCenter's  sale  of  Harmon  land  recorded  within  income  from  unconsolidated  affiliates,  and  income  from  unconsolidated affiliates related to investments in real estate ventures.

2a3a6273-01c8-4c1f-a58d-9533b4bab7eb.label: text_chunk
2a3a6273-01c8-4c1f-a58d-9533b4bab7eb.text:
"Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR"  is  the  Company's  reportable  segment  GAAP  measure,  which  management  utilizes  as  the primary  profit  measure  for  its  reportable  segments  and  underlying  operating  segments.

 Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  is  a measure defined as earnings before interest and other non-operating income (expense), taxes, depreciation and amortization,

389dbb06-c7dd-4120-a597-708606cf6aab.label: text_chunk
389dbb06-c7dd-4120-a597-708606cf6aab.text: preopening and start-up expenses, property transactions, net, gain on REIT transactions, net, rent expense related to triple-net operating leases and ground leases, income from unconsolidated affiliates related to investments in real estate ventures, and also  excludes  gain  on  consolidation  of  CityCenter,  net,  gain  related  to  CityCenter's  sale  of  Harmon  land  recorded  within income from unconsolidated affiliates,

92fc7d59-857c-4d5b-975a-cafe37624389.label: text_chunk
92fc7d59-857c-4d5b-975a-cafe37624389.text: corporate expense and stock compensation expense, which are not allocated to each operating segment, and rent expense related to the master lease with MGP that eliminated in consolidation.

2fec5bcd-c06e-4d8c-908d-d6d9bf11d0a9.label: text_chunk
2fec5bcd-c06e-4d8c-908d-d6d9bf11d0a9.text:
"Same-Store  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR"  is  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  further  adjusted  to  exclude  the  Adjusted Property EBITDAR of acquired operating segments from the date of acquisition through the end of the reporting period and
Page 8 of 15

0db73b24-fe5b-4bd3-8371-2f516aa69b84.label: text_chunk
0db73b24-fe5b-4bd3-8371-2f516aa69b84.text:
to  exclude  the  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  of  disposed  operating  segments  from  the  beginning  of  the  reporting  period through  the  date  of  disposition.

  Accordingly,  the  Company  has  excluded  the  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  of  The Cosmopolitan  for  periods  subsequent  to  its  acquisition  on  May  17,

295f297b-4cdf-4663-934e-95687ff813d8.label: text_chunk
295f297b-4cdf-4663-934e-95687ff813d8.text:
2022, Aria  for  periods  subsequent  to  its  acquisition  on September 27, 2021, and The Mirage for the periods prior to its disposition on December 19, 2022 in Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR for the periods indicated, as applicable.

Same-Store Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  is  a  non-GAAP  measure  and  is  presented  solely  as

6eef23c3-2f3d-4a26-9b8e-32d86535a675.label: text_chunk
6eef23c3-2f3d-4a26-9b8e-32d86535a675.text: a  supplemental  disclosure  to reported  GAAP  measures  because  management  believes  this  measure  is  useful  in  providing  meaningful  period-to-period comparisons  of  the  results  of  the  Company's  operations  for  operating  segments  that  were  consolidated  for  the  full  period presented  to  assist  users  of  the  financial  statements  in  reviewing  operating  performance  over

ebce9f9d-e540-47dd-beba-2829a8e7dadf.label: text_chunk
ebce9f9d-e540-47dd-beba-2829a8e7dadf.text:
time.

  Same-Store  Adjusted Property  EBITDAR  should not  be  viewed  as  a measure of  overall  operating  performance,  considered  in  isolation,  or as  an alternative  to  the  Company's  reportable  segment  GAAP  measure  or  net  income,  or  as  an  alternative  to  any  other  measure determined in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles,

f8d9e138-9219-4c76-b202-80924ef6bccd.label: text_chunk
f8d9e138-9219-4c76-b202-80924ef6bccd.text:
because this measure is not presented on a  GAAP basis, and is provided for the limited purposes discussed herein.

 In addition, Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR may not be defined in the same manner by all companies and, as a result, may not be comparable to similarly titled non-GAAP financial measures of other companies, and such differences may be material.

3323f561-5de3-4293-84bb-303ad033c38f.label: text_chunk
3323f561-5de3-4293-84bb-303ad033c38f.text: A reconciliation of the Company's reportable segment  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  GAAP  measure  to  Same-Store  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  is  included  in  the financial schedules in this release.

472534c1-99c2-4a32-8a57-7c872305d0ab.label: text_chunk
472534c1-99c2-4a32-8a57-7c872305d0ab.text: "Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Adjusted Property EBITDAR" and "Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Same-Store  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR"  are  supplemental  non-GAAP  financial  measures,  that,

8f00ab44-6d7c-4ac8-9520-9210f7b0c2cf.label: text_chunk
8f00ab44-6d7c-4ac8-9520-9210f7b0c2cf.text: in addition  to  the  reasons  described  above  for  the  presentation  of  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  and  Same-Store  Adjusted Property EBITDAR, are presented to adjust for the impact of certain variances in table games win percentages compared to the mid-point of the expected ranges.

59612fdd-2165-485d-94c1-8cfb2f3bdc6e.label: text_chunk
59612fdd-2165-485d-94c1-8cfb2f3bdc6e.text: Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Adjusted Property EBITDAR and Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR are calculated by applying a win percentage of 30.0% for Baccarat and 21.0% for non-Baccarat games to the respective table games drops for the quarter, which represents the mid-point of the expected ranges of 25.0% to 35.

c80e918e-eb19-40c1-8b76-face8cc9e035.label: text_chunk
c80e918e-eb19-40c1-8b76-face8cc9e035.text: 0% for Baccarat and 19.0% to 23.0% for non-Baccarat at the Las Vegas Strip Resorts properties. Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR is Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR adjusted to exclude the hold adjustments related to such acquired and disposed  operating  segments  for  the  respective  periods.

2c4cbfc9-3c27-442e-b590-8cf079a25116.label: text_chunk
2c4cbfc9-3c27-442e-b590-8cf079a25116.text: Table  Games  Hold  Adjusted  Las  Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Adjusted Property EBITDAR and Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR are also  adjusted  for  the  gaming  taxes,  bad  debt  expense,  discounts  and  other  incentives  that  would  have  been  incurred  or avoided when applying the win percentages noted above to the respective gaming volumes.

ee1b20bb-9472-40b6-903c-214974d90714.label: text_chunk
ee1b20bb-9472-40b6-903c-214974d90714.text: Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Adjusted  Property  EBITDAR  and  Table  Games  Hold  Adjusted  Las  Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR should not be viewed as a measure of overall operating performance, considered in isolation, or as an alternative to the Company's reportable segment GAAP measure or net income,

a01660a2-5a92-4b47-b85e-535a045ff61f.label: text_chunk
a01660a2-5a92-4b47-b85e-535a045ff61f.text: or to any other measure determined in accordance  with  generally  accepted  accounting  principles,  because  this  measure  is  not  presented  on  a  GAAP  basis,  and  is provided for the limited purposes discussed herein.

144dea4a-1acb-41b0-a749-ae3eded582b0.label: text_chunk
144dea4a-1acb-41b0-a749-ae3eded582b0.text: In addition, Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Adjusted Property EBITDAR and Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR may not be defined in the same manner by all companies and, as a result, may not be comparable to similarly titled non-GAAP financials measures of other companies, and such differences may be material.

16c7dbc9-d96e-4981-a77c-ec9c64b5eb64.label: text_chunk
16c7dbc9-d96e-4981-a77c-ec9c64b5eb64.text:
A reconciliation of the Company's reportable segment Adjusted Property EBITDAR GAAP measure to Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Adjusted Property EBITDAR is included in the financial schedules in this release.

Adjusted  EBITDAR  information  is  a  non-GAAP  measure  that  is  a  valuation  metric,  should  not  be  used  as  an  operating metric,

0b5f95c0-59bf-4172-b4ce-6d7286aef59a.label: text_chunk
0b5f95c0-59bf-4172-b4ce-6d7286aef59a.text: and is presented solely as a supplemental disclosure to reported GAAP measures because management believes this measure  is  widely  used  by  analysts,  lenders,  financial  institutions,  and  investors  as  a  principal  basis  for  the  valuation  of gaming  companies.

68c14ee1-5f31-499c-bb7d-db299702909f.label: text_chunk
68c14ee1-5f31-499c-bb7d-db299702909f.text: Management  believes  that  while  items  excluded  from Adjusted  EBITDAR  may  be  recurring  in  nature and should not be disregarded in evaluation of the Company's earnings performance, it is useful to exclude such items when analyzing current results and trends. Also, management believes excluded items may not relate specifically to current trends or  be  indicative  of  future  results.

6b022696-3685-40ac-9333-d9ece5269d69.label: text_chunk
6b022696-3685-40ac-9333-d9ece5269d69.text: For  example,  preopening  and  start-up  expenses  will  be  significantly  different  in  periods when the Company is developing and constructing a major expansion project and will depend on where the current period lies within the development cycle, as well as the size and scope of the project(s).

8d7e9b8e-4865-412d-99a1-041e8269c6a0.label: text_chunk
8d7e9b8e-4865-412d-99a1-041e8269c6a0.text:
Property transactions, net includes normal recurring  disposals,  gains  and  losses  on  sales  of  assets  related  to  specific  assets  within  the  Company's  resorts,  but  also
Page 9 of 15

81b8bd59-7058-4b74-9508-a35390f49af6.label: text_chunk
81b8bd59-7058-4b74-9508-a35390f49af6.text: includes  gains  or  losses  on  sales  of  an  entire operating  resort  or  a  group of  resorts  and impairment  charges  on  entire  asset groups  or  investments  in  unconsolidated  affiliates,  which  may  not  be  comparable  period  over  period.  In  addition, management excludes rent expense related to triple-net operating leases and ground leases.

68b020ae-25aa-400f-a783-b87543a6068a.label: text_chunk
68b020ae-25aa-400f-a783-b87543a6068a.text: Management believes excluding rent expense related to triple-net operating leases and ground leases provides useful information to analysts, lenders, financial institutions,  and  investors  when  valuing  the  Company,  as  well  as  comparing  the  Company's  results  to  other  gaming companies,

7b82eeff-58c2-44b9-9ca5-83abf0eaabe2.label: text_chunk
7b82eeff-58c2-44b9-9ca5-83abf0eaabe2.text:
without regard to differences in capital structure and leasing arrangements since the operations of other gaming companies  may  or  may  not  include  triple-net  operating  leases  or  ground  leases.

  However,  as  discussed  herein,  Adjusted EBITDAR should not be viewed as a measure of overall operating performance, an indicator of the Company's performance, considered in isolation, or construed as an alternative to operating income or net income,

09a572d6-f357-4bb8-afcc-04d36b62165b.label: text_chunk
09a572d6-f357-4bb8-afcc-04d36b62165b.text: or as an alternative to cash flows from operating activities, as a measure of liquidity, or as an alternative to any other measure determined in accordance with generally  accepted  accounting  principles,  because  this  measure  is  not  presented  on  a  GAAP  basis  and  excludes  certain expenses, including the rent expense related to triple-net operating leases and ground leases, and is provided for the limited purposes  discussed  herein.

8fcc72e6-c816-408f-b0b5-72708599bab2.label: text_chunk
8fcc72e6-c816-408f-b0b5-72708599bab2.text: In  addition,  other  companies  in  the  gaming  and  hospitality  industries  that  report  Adjusted EBITDAR may calculate Adjusted EBITDAR in a different manner and such differences may be material. The Company has significant uses of cash flows, including capital expenditures, interest payments, taxes, real estate triple-net lease and ground lease payments, and debt principal repayments, which are not reflected in Adjusted EBITDAR.

2f21bc06-5a00-4838-8d65-90577beb383c.label: text_chunk
2f21bc06-5a00-4838-8d65-90577beb383c.text:
A reconciliation of GAAP net income (loss) to Adjusted EBITDAR is included in the financial schedules in this release.

to  adjust Las Vegas Strip Resorts net revenues for the impact of certain variances in table games win percentages compared to the mid- point  of  the  expected  ranges,  as  described  herein.

  Table  Games  Hold  Adjusted  Las  Vegas  Strip  Resorts  Same-Store  Net Revenues  excludes  the  net  revenues  of  acquired  operating  segments  from  the  date  of  acquisition  through  the

697bb02a-2d76-4630-9d40-8102a5a5735c.text: end  of  the reporting period and the net revenues of disposed operating segments from the beginning of the reporting period through the date of disposition and also excludes the hold adjustment related to such acquired and disposed operating segments for the respective periods.

5a045e8c-66e2-4c50-ac8b-75271142bee9.label: text_chunk
5a045e8c-66e2-4c50-ac8b-75271142bee9.text: Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Net Revenues and Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Net Revenues are also adjusted for the discounts and other incentives that would have been incurred  or  avoided  when  applying  the  win  percentages  described  herein  to  the  respective  gaming  volumes.

369dfa8e-9a1c-44b2-b82e-244b9adff692.label: text_chunk
369dfa8e-9a1c-44b2-b82e-244b9adff692.text: Management believes Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Net Revenues and Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts  Same-Store  Net  Revenues  present  consistent  measures  in  providing  period-to-period  comparisons  and  are  useful measures in assisting investors in evaluating the Company's operating performance,

a3a90186-1e1d-4bea-92db-e2696b48ba8f.text: and that Table Games Hold Adjusted Las Vegas Strip Resorts Same-Store Net Revenues is useful in providing meaningful period-to-period comparisons of the results of the Company's operations for operating segments that were consolidated by the Company for the full period presented to assist investors in reviewing the Company's operating performance over time.

About MGM Resorts International

MGM  Resorts  International  (NYSE:  MGM)  is  an  S&P 500® global gaming and entertainment company with national and international locations featuring best-in-class hotels and casinos, state-of-the-art meetings and conference spaces, incredible live  and  theatrical  entertainment  experiences,  and  an  extensive  array  of  restaurant,  nightlife  and  retail  offerings.

9c4e1369-d6f7-4dc3-ab07-5026b837c1ab.label: text_chunk
9c4e1369-d6f7-4dc3-ab07-5026b837c1ab.text: MGM Resorts  creates  immersive,  iconic  experiences  through  its  suite  of  Las  Vegas-inspired  brands.  The  MGM  Resorts  portfolio encompasses 32 unique hotel and gaming destinations globally, including some of the most recognizable resort brands in the industry.

2a536364-7a8a-4081-80de-c82157287501.label: text_chunk
2a536364-7a8a-4081-80de-c82157287501.text: The Company's 50/50 venture, BetMGM, LLC, offers sports betting and online gaming in North America through market-leading  brands,  including  BetMGM  and  partypoker,  and  the  Company's  subsidiary,  LeoVegas  AB,  offers  sports betting  and  online  gaming  through  market-leading  brands  in  several  jurisdictions  throughout  Europe.

d4ce2002-0782-4e88-a64b-573a6b53a1c2.label: text_chunk
d4ce2002-0782-4e88-a64b-573a6b53a1c2.text: The  Company  is currently pursuing targeted expansion in Asia through the integrated resort opportunity in Japan.

e7b5263a-2929-4732-af3d-fbbcd9278bac.label: text_chunk
e7b5263a-2929-4732-af3d-fbbcd9278bac.text: Through its “Focused on What  Matters:  Embracing  Humanity  and  Protecting  the  Planet”  philosophy,  MGM  Resorts  commits  to  creating  a  more sustainable  future,  while  striving  to  make  a  bigger  difference  in  the  lives  of  its  employees,  guests,  and  in  the  communities where  it  operates.

bfeb2479-1bb9-425b-9192-860bb4f7f7b8.label: text_chunk
bfeb2479-1bb9-425b-9192-860bb4f7f7b8.text: The  global  employees  of  MGM  Resorts  are  proud  of  their  company  for  being  recognized  as  one  of FORTUNE® Magazine's World's Most Admired Companies®. For more information, please visit us at www.mgmresorts.com. Please also connect with us @MGMResortsIntl on Twitter as well as Facebook and Instagram.

The relationships outlined involve two key financial metrics: **Earnings per Share Basic** and **Earnings per Share Diluted**. 

1. **Earnings per Share Basic** is derived from the **Weighted Average Common Shares Outstanding Basic**. This calculation involves dividing the net income by the weighted average number of common shares outstanding, providing a measure of profitability on a basic level.

2. **Earnings per Share Diluted** is calculated from the **Weighted Average Common Shares Outstanding Diluted**. Similar to the basic earnings per share, this metric also divides net income by the weighted average number of shares, but it accounts for potential dilution from convertible securities, options, and other sources.

In summary, both earnings per share metrics are fundamentally linked to their respective weighted average common shares outstanding, with the basic version reflecting straightforward profitability and the diluted version offering a more conservative view by considering potential share dilution.

5. **Entertainment Offerings**:

Overall, the relationships highlight the financial metrics that define the operational success of Las Vegas Strip Resorts, their compliance with accounting standards, and their role within the regional gaming and entertainment landscape.

The relationships outlined highlight various financial impacts on earnings and equity within a corporate context. Here’s a summary of the key entities and their interrelations:

1. **Non-operating Items and Diluted Earnings Per Share (EPS)**: Non-operating items can significantly influence the calculation of diluted EPS, as they may contribute to the overall earnings or losses reported by a company.

2. **Property Transactions and EPS**: Property transactions can adversely affect EPS by decreasing earnings, as shown in the adjustments made when reconciling EPS to Adjusted EPS.

3. **Table Games Win Percentages and Adjusted Property EBITDAR**: Fluctuations in table games win percentages can impact Adjusted Property EBITDAR, as changes in gaming revenue directly influence earnings before adjustments.

4. **Operating Lease Liabilities and Stockholders' Equity**: Operating lease liabilities represent future obligations that can diminish stockholders' equity, thereby reducing the overall equity available to shareholders.

5. **Operating Income (Loss) and Preopening and Start-up Expenses**: Preopening and start-up expenses can negatively affect operating income (loss), as these costs are incurred prior to revenue generation, potentially leading to a loss in operating income.

Overall, these relationships illustrate how various financial elements interact, affecting earnings per share, equity, and overall financial performance within a company.

The relationships outlined involve key financial concepts related to tax liabilities and benefits, particularly focusing on foreign tax credit carryforwards and net operating losses.

1. **Foreign Tax Credit Carryforwards and Taxable Foreign-Sourced Income**: Foreign tax credit carryforwards can be utilized to offset taxes owed on taxable foreign-sourced income. This mechanism effectively reduces the overall tax liability for taxpayers, allowing them to manage their tax obligations more efficiently.

2. **Net Operating Losses and Tax Benefits**: Net operating losses (NOLs) can provide significant tax benefits when carried back to offset taxable income in years with higher tax rates. This strategy helps in reducing the overall tax liability by leveraging past losses against current or future income.

3. **Carrying Back Net Operating Losses**: Specifically, net operating losses incurred in 2018 can be carried back to offset taxable income in subsequent years, such as 2019 and 2020. This provision allows taxpayers to reclaim taxes paid in previous years, further alleviating their tax burden.

Overall, these relationships highlight the strategic use of tax credits and losses to optimize tax liabilities, emphasizing the importance of understanding carryforwards and carrybacks in tax planning.

The description emphasizes that the present value is derived by discounting future payments, ensuring that the financial assessment reflects current value.

In summary, these relationships illustrate a fundamental financial principle where future lease obligations are evaluated in present terms through a discounting process, ensuring accurate financial reporting and analysis.

community_summary.351:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Financial Assessments and Reporting**:
   - The Company compares carrying value against fair value to evaluate its financial standing and presents financial statements as of December 31, 2018, which reflect its financial position. It also compares these statements with those from December 31, 2017, to illustrate changes over the year. The audit report was issued on February 27, 2019, marking the completion of the audit process.

2. **Tax Responsibilities**:
   - Members of the Company are responsible for federal income taxes as it is treated as a partnership. The Company recognizes deferred income tax assets and adjusts its valuation allowance on FTC carryovers, indicating active tax management.

3. **Acquisitions and Investments**:
   - The Company has entered into an acquisition agreement with MGP to acquire Northfield's operating assets and has also invested in CityCenter and MGM China. It exercises significant influence over its unconsolidated affiliates and evaluates these investments for impairment.

4. **Operational Relationships**:
   - The Company provides goods and services to gaming patrons and issues credit to casino customers after thorough background checks. It also acts as an agent for transferring goods and services and allocates revenue from casino wager transactions.

5. **Financial Obligations and Liabilities**:
   - The Company has various financial obligations, including accounts payable, lease obligations, and obligations related to MGM China and the senior credit facility. It has recorded long-term obligations and has an estimated fair value of $1.58 billion for its long-term debt.

6. **Legal and Compliance Matters**:
   - The Company is involved in legal actions in Nevada and engages in mediation with plaintiffs' counsel. It maintains insurance coverage and is self-insured for workers' compensation, indicating a proactive approach to managing liabilities.

7. **Stock and Equity Management**:
   - The Company repurchased 41 million shares, spending $1.3 billion in the process. It has outstanding Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and recognizes its equity interests in MGM China and CityCenter.

8. **Operational Developments**:
   - The Company is responsible for renovations at Park MGM and holds a master lease for MGM National Harbor. It may consider undeveloped land for future gaming facility development.

9. **Financial Reporting and Compliance**:
   - The Company is audited by Deloitte & Touche LLP and is compliant with applicable covenants as of December 31, 2018. It is also a party to various financial agreements, including credit agreements and indentures.

10. **Future Projections**:
    - The Company anticipates an increase in operating fees to $1.5 million starting in 2021, reflecting its ongoing financial planning and projections.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and operational dynamics of the Company, highlighting its financial management, tax responsibilities, investment strategies, and compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks.

community_summary.394:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Credit and Gaming Receivables**: The extension of credit to customers can lead to gaming receivables, which are amounts owed for gaming services. Widespread credit extension may complicate the collection of these receivables.

2. **Interest Rates and Borrowings**: An increase in interest rates raises the cost of borrowings, resulting in higher expenses for borrowers.

3. **Capital and Credit Market Conditions and Indebtedness**: Favorable capital and credit market conditions facilitate borrowing and refinancing, while adverse conditions can hinder the ability to manage existing debt.

4. **Operational Ability and Indebtedness**: The capacity to fund operations and raise capital is closely tied to the level of indebtedness; higher debt limits financial flexibility.

5. **Cash Flow and Working Capital**: Cash flow directly affects working capital, as reduced operational cash flow limits available funds for managing working capital.

6. **Affected Properties and Business Operations**: The condition of affected properties can significantly impact a business's operations and financial health.

7. **Development Risks and Future Projects**: Risks associated with development and construction can lead to increased costs and delays, affecting future project planning and execution.

8. **Risks and Operational Results**: Various risks can negatively influence operational results, potentially leading to financial losses or reduced profitability.

9. **Cyber-attacks and Computer Systems**: Cyber-attacks threaten the functionality of computer systems, potentially causing service interruptions for customers.

10. **Corporate Financials and Segment Revenues**: The corporate and other financial category can influence reportable segment net revenues by including costs and income that affect overall financial performance.

11. **Fixed-Rate Debt and Interest Rates**: Changes in interest rates can affect the management and cost of fixed-rate debt, impacting future earnings and cash flow.

12. **Translation Adjustments**: Balance sheet and income statement accounts are subject to translation adjustments due to currency fluctuations, affecting financial reporting.

13. **Income from Affiliates and Non-operating Items**: Non-operating items can impact income from unconsolidated affiliates, influencing overall financial performance.

14. **Consolidation Adjustments**: The corporate and other financial category is affected by eliminations during consolidation, which adjusts expenses and revenues.

15. **Noncontrolling Interests and Net Income**: Net income attributable to noncontrolling interests is deducted from total net income to reflect the portion not owned by the parent company.

16. **Financing Activities and Cash Equivalents**: Net cash from financing activities affects the liquidity position by influencing the overall cash and cash equivalents available.

17. **Operating Income and Other Items**: Operating income can be affected by other net items, which may include additional income or expenses impacting overall financial performance.

18. **Cash Flow Calculations**: The balance at the beginning of the period serves as the starting point for calculating net changes in cash and cash equivalents.

19. **Gaming License and Operations**: The status of a gaming license directly impacts the ability to conduct gaming operations, depending on whether it is limited, conditioned, suspended, or revoked.

20. **Provision for Doubtful Accounts and Receivables**: The provision for doubtful accounts estimates uncollectible receivables, affecting the company's financial health.

21. **Discontinued Operations and Net Income**: Losses from discontinued operations negatively affect net income from continuing operations, representing a financial detriment.

22. **Promotional Allowances and Casino Revenues**: Promotional allowances can offset casino revenues based on performance obligations.

23. **Cash Flows from Discontinued Operations**: Discontinued operations impact reported cash flows, reflecting inflows and outflows from inactive business segments. 

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and their implications for financial performance and operational integrity across various entities and sectors.
community_summary.395: The relationship between ASU 2016-15 and ASC 230 is characterized by ASU 2016-15 amending the guidance provided by ASC 230. community_summary.396:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Attribution and Noncontrolling Interests**: The relationship between attribution and noncontrolling interests highlights that net income is calculated after considering noncontrolling interests, which represent the share of net income attributable to minority shareholders in a subsidiary. This is crucial for understanding how minority interests impact the financial performance reported by the parent company.

2. **Comprehensive Income and Noncontrolling Interests**: Comprehensive income is influenced by the portion attributable to noncontrolling interests, indicating that earnings not owned by the parent company are included in the overall comprehensive income calculation. This relationship emphasizes the importance of recognizing minority interests in assessing the total earnings of a company.

3. **Net Income and Tax Benefits**: The benefit provision for income taxes plays a significant role in net income, as it reduces taxable income, thereby increasing net income after taxes. This relationship underscores the impact of tax strategies on a company's profitability.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of net income, comprehensive income, and the effects of noncontrolling interests and tax benefits, highlighting the complexities involved in financial reporting and the importance of accurately reflecting minority interests in financial statements.
community_summary.397:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Reputation and Customers**: A business's reputation significantly influences customer engagement, enhancing trust and loyalty, which directly affects transaction willingness.

2. **Estimates and Assumptions**: In accounting, estimates are closely tied to assumptions; any changes in assumptions can lead to revised estimates, highlighting their interdependence.

3. **Convention Customers and Customer Segments**: Convention customers are a subset of customer segments that can shape marketing strategies and overall business outcomes.

4. **Operational Performance and Financial Performance**: There is a direct correlation between operational performance and financial performance, where improvements in efficiency can lead to increased revenue and profitability.

5. **Global Economy and Human Capital Leadership**: The global economy necessitates that organizations adapt their workforce strategies to maintain competitiveness in a dynamic economic environment.

6. **Economic Conditions and Capital Markets**: Economic fluctuations significantly impact capital and credit market conditions, influencing investor confidence, interest rates, and credit availability.

7. **Market Risks and Foreign Currency Exchange Rates**: Changes in foreign currency exchange rates represent a market risk that can induce financial volatility for companies involved in international trade.

8. **Union Messaging and Customer Demand**: Adverse publicity from union activities can negatively affect a company's reputation, thereby influencing customer demand and purchasing decisions.

9. **Weighted Average Common Shares and Dividends**: The number of weighted average common shares directly affects the calculation of dividends declared per common share, as dividends are distributed based on outstanding shares.

10. **Average Daily Rate (ADR) and Complimentary Rooms**: The ADR is influenced by the rates of complimentary rooms, which are factored into the average price of occupied rooms.

11. **Investing Cash Flows and Strategic Capital Investments**: Fluctuations in investing cash flows are directly linked to decisions regarding strategic capital investments, indicating the impact of investment strategies on cash flow.

12. **Aged Account Balances and Customer Financial Condition**: The likelihood of collecting aged account balances is influenced by the customer's financial condition, with better financial health increasing the chances of debt settlement.

13. **Diluted Net Income Per Share and Property/Equipment**: The value of diluted net income per share is affected by the company's property and equipment, which contribute to operational capacity and profitability.

14. **Key Persons and Institutional Investors**: Key individuals play a crucial role in shaping the strategies and decisions of institutional investors, underscoring their significance in the investment landscape.

15. **Interest Rate Swaps and Property Transactions**: Changes in the fair value of interest rate swaps can impact property transactions by altering financing costs, thereby influencing investment decisions in real estate.

This summary encapsulates the interconnectedness of various entities and their influence on business operations, financial performance, and market dynamics.

community_summary.399:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Net Revenues** contribute to **Operating Income**: Net Revenues, representing total income from sales, are essential for calculating Operating Income as they provide the necessary revenue to cover operating expenses.

2. **Construction** contributes to **Other Accrued Liabilities**: Ongoing construction activities can lead to the recognition of expenses that result in accrued liabilities, which are not yet paid.

3. **Non-Casino Revenue** contributes to **Reportable Segment Net Revenues**: Non-casino revenue is a significant component of total income generated outside of casino operations, enhancing overall reportable segment net revenues.

4. **Cash and Cash Equivalents** contribute to **Total Current Assets**: These liquid assets are crucial as they can be quickly converted to cash, forming part of the total current assets.

5. **Accounts Receivable** contribute to **Total Current Assets**: Accounts receivable, representing amounts owed to the company expected to be collected within a year, are included in total current assets.

6. **Capital in Earnings** contributes to **Stockholders' Equity**: This reflects retained earnings that enhance the overall equity of the company.

7. **Net Operating Loss Carryforward** contributes to **Deferred Tax Assets**: These carryforwards can be utilized to reduce future taxable income based on past losses, serving as deferred tax assets.

8. **Unrealized Gain on Cash Flow Hedges** contributes to **Other Comprehensive Income (Loss)**: This gain is included in Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), indicating its impact on overall financial results.

9. **Proceeds from Issuance of Redeemable Noncontrolling Interests** contribute to **Net Cash Provided by Financing Activities**: These proceeds represent a source of cash inflow, enhancing net cash from financing activities.

10. **Net Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations** contributes to **Net Income (Loss)**: This component reflects the ongoing profitability of the company and is included in the total net income calculation.

11. **Income (Loss) from Discontinued Operations** contributes to **Net Income (Loss)**: This accounts for profits or losses from ceased operations, impacting the overall net income.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of financial metrics and how various components contribute to the overall financial health and reporting of a company.

2. **Subconcession contract** affects **MGM Grand Paradise**: The terms and renewal of the subconcession contract are vital for MGM Grand Paradise's revenue generation capabilities.

3. **Income Tax** affects **Foreign Withholding Taxes**: Changes in income tax regulations can influence foreign withholding taxes, affecting international business operations.

4. **Air Services** affects **Las Vegas**: The availability of air services is essential for travel to Las Vegas, directly impacting visitor numbers.

5. **High-end Gaming** affects **Earnings**: Variations in high-end gaming results can significantly alter earnings, establishing a direct correlation between the two.

6. **Global geopolitical events** affect **Air Travel**: Such events can deter customers from traveling due to safety concerns, impacting air travel significantly.

7. **Impairment Charges** affect **Unconsolidated Affiliates**: Impairment charges can negatively impact the financial performance of unconsolidated affiliates by reducing asset values.

8. **Preopening Property Depreciation** affects **Operating Income**: This depreciation represents a cost that reduces profitability before the property generates revenue.

9. **Stock Compensation** affects **Corporate**: The structure of employee compensation through stock options impacts corporate financial statements.

10. **Allowance for Doubtful Accounts** affects **Casino Accounts Receivable**: This allowance influences the valuation of casino receivables by estimating uncollectible amounts.

11. **Benefit for Income Taxes** affects **Net Income**: Tax benefits reduce the overall tax burden, thereby increasing reported net income.

12. **Taxes** affect **MGP Dividend**: Tax obligations can influence the dividends distributed to shareholders.

13. **Foreign Operations** affect **Income**: Negative income from foreign operations can lead to overall losses before taxes.

14. **Repatriation of Foreign Earnings** affects **Foreign Tax Credit**: The repatriation process can influence the foreign tax credit available based on taxes paid abroad.

15. **Income Before Income Taxes** affects **Benefit for Income Taxes**: This income level determines taxable income and potential tax benefits.

16. **Benefit (Provision) for Income Taxes** affects **Net Income**: This provision is deducted from income before taxes to calculate final net income.

17. **Loss on Sale of Mandarin Oriental** affects **Net Income**: Losses from this sale directly reduce the overall profit reported.

### Summary of Relationships Involving the Operating Partnership

1. **Financial Reporting**: The Operating Partnership's financial status, including outstanding senior notes, is reported as of **December 31, 2018**, establishing a temporal relationship with this date.

2. **Management and Operations**: The Operating Partnership manages the operations and financial aspects of **CityCenter** and the real estate assets of **The Mirage**, indicating its role in overseeing significant assets within MGM Resorts International's portfolio.

3. **Borrowing and Credit Facilities**: The Operating Partnership is identified as a borrower under its senior secured credit facility and has borrowed **$2.1 billion** in 2016. It also utilizes a **Term Loan B Facility** for financing, with a repayment timeline extending to **March 2025**. The partnership has made repayments, including a permanent repayment of **$19 million**.

4. **Corporate Structure**: The Operating Partnership operates as a subsidiary of the **Company**, reflecting its position within a larger corporate framework.

5. **Financial Transactions**: The Operating Partnership has sold its investment in **Grand Victoria**, affecting its financial status, and issued **$266 million** in common stock to fund the acquisition of **Empire City**. It also pays distributions to **MGP** and has issued **19.6 million units** to MGP, indicating various financial transactions between these entities.

6. **Compliance and Limitations**: The Operating Partnership is in compliance with its credit facility covenants, which are crucial for maintaining its financial health. However, it faces limitations on dividend payments due to the terms of its credit facilities.

7. **Guarantees and Exclusions**: The Operating Partnership guarantees its credit facility through its restricted subsidiaries and excludes **MGM National Harbor** from its secured assets, highlighting specific asset management strategies.

8. **Co-Issuer Relationships**: **MGP Finance Co-Issuer, Inc.** acts as a co-issuer of the senior notes issued by the Operating Partnership, establishing a financial relationship.

9. **Future Financial Actions**: The Operating Partnership plans to execute financial swaps effective on **December 31, 2019**, indicating ongoing financial strategies.

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and financial dynamics involving the Operating Partnership, emphasizing its management roles, borrowing activities, corporate structure, and compliance with financial agreements.

The relationships surrounding Adjusted EBITDA highlight its significance as a financial metric for evaluating a company's operational performance. 

1. **Expected Benefits**: Adjusted EBITDA is projected to increase by **$200 million** due to various initiatives, with an additional **$100 million** anticipated by the end of 2021. This indicates a strong potential for improved financial performance as a result of strategic actions.

2. **Impact of Corporate Expenses**: There is a noted decrease in Adjusted EBITDA attributed to rising **corporate expenses**, which underscores the direct financial impact of operational costs on overall earnings.

3. **Role in Financial Reporting**: Adjusted EBITDA serves as a **supplemental disclosure** to GAAP measures, providing additional context to standard financial statements. This highlights its importance in offering a clearer picture of the company's financial health.

4. **Comparison with Operating Income**: Adjusted EBITDA is often compared to **Operating Income**, as both are performance measures. However, Adjusted EBITDA excludes certain non-recurring items, offering a different perspective on the company's earnings.

5. **Evaluation Metric**: Overall, Adjusted EBITDA is a crucial metric used to assess how effectively the company generates earnings from its operations, reflecting its operational efficiency and financial viability.

In summary, Adjusted EBITDA is a vital financial indicator that not only reflects expected growth but also the challenges posed by corporate expenses, while providing essential insights into the company's performance relative to GAAP standards and other financial metrics.

The Mississippi Gaming Commission (MGC) plays a crucial regulatory role in overseeing casino operations and ensuring compliance with gaming laws. Key relationships and responsibilities include:

1. **Approval Requirements**: The MGC mandates that stockholders of casino licensees obtain necessary licenses and approvals before they can receive profits from the casino, emphasizing the Commission's authority in financial oversight.

2. **Suitability Findings**: The MGC may require individuals with significant relationships to casino operations to undergo a "finding of suitability" process, which assesses their qualifications and compliance with regulatory standards.

3. **Application Process**: The finding of suitability is a structured process managed by the MGC to ensure that individuals meet the necessary criteria to participate in the gaming industry.

4. **Regulatory Authority**: The MGC has the power to impose additional requirements on entities governed by the Mississippi Act, showcasing its ability to enforce and modify regulations as needed.

5. **Waivers**: The Commission can grant waivers, allowing for specific exemptions from prior approval requirements, indicating flexibility in its regulatory approach.

6. **Investigative Requirements**: Entities seeking to acquire control of casino operations must undergo an investigation by the MGC, which is part of the approval process to ensure responsible management.

7. **Oversight of Licensees**: The MGC holds the authority to limit, condition, suspend, or revoke the licenses of casino licensees based on their adherence to gaming laws, reinforcing its role in maintaining industry integrity.

Overall, the Mississippi Gaming Commission serves as a pivotal regulatory body, ensuring that all stakeholders in the gaming industry comply with established laws and standards, thereby safeguarding the integrity of the gaming environment in Mississippi.

community_summary.647:
**Summary of Relationships Involving MGM Grand Paradise**

1. **MGM Grand Paradise and Macau Government**:
   - MGM Grand Paradise operates under a subconcession agreement with the Macau government, allowing it to provide gaming and entertainment services. This relationship is time-bound, with the subconcession set to expire in 2020. Upon termination, all casino premises and equipment will be transferred to the Macau government without compensation, unless the subconcession is extended or legislation amended. MGM Grand Paradise is entitled to compensation if the government exercises its redemption rights. Additionally, MGM Grand Paradise must notify the government of any significant changes regarding shareholders or key employees and is required to submit regular financial reports to comply with gaming regulations.

2. **Financial Relationships**:
   - MGM Grand Paradise and MGM China are co-borrowers under a credit facility, sharing the responsibility for repayment. MGM Grand Paradise also distributes its gaming profits to MGM China, its shareholder. The credit facility is secured by MGM Grand Paradise's interest in the MGM Cotai land concession. Furthermore, MGM Grand Paradise benefits from a corporate tax exemption, avoiding the 12% complementary tax on gaming profits, and has a tax agreement with the Macau government to settle this tax through a flat annual payment.

3. **Operational Dependencies**:
   - MGM Grand Paradise relies heavily on gaming promoters to attract customers and generate revenue. It is actively working to strengthen these relationships, although there is uncertainty about maintaining them. The casino's operations are also subject to regulatory restrictions, including a prohibition on delegating management to a third-party company.

4. **Corporate Structure**:
   - MGM Grand Paradise is a subsidiary of MGM Resorts International, indicating a corporate relationship that supports its operational framework. The casino has received a land concession from the Macau government to develop a second resort under the MGM Cotai Land Contract.

5. **Comparative Context**:
   - MGM Grand Paradise is often compared to major gaming resorts on the Las Vegas Strip, emphasizing its significance within the broader gaming industry landscape.

Overall, the relationships involving MGM Grand Paradise highlight its operational dependencies, regulatory obligations, and financial arrangements, all of which are intricately linked to its partnership with the Macau government and its corporate structure.

### Summary of Relationships Involving MGP

1. **Consolidation with Operating Partnership**: MGP consolidates its financial results with the Operating Partnership, indicating a unified reporting of their financial performance.

5. **Beneficiary of Leased Properties**: MGP benefits from expenses related to leased properties, as it owns the associated facilities.

6. **Transaction Approval Policy**: MGP requires approval for transactions exceeding $25 million, indicating a structured approach to financial governance.

7. **Potential Conflicts of Interest**: MGP and its affiliates may face conflicts of interest, particularly in decisions impacting shareholders.

11. **Issuance of Operating Partnership Units**: MGP issued units of the Operating Partnership as part of its financial operations and received 13.2 million units in connection with a public offering.

12. **Dividends to Shareholders and Subsidiaries**: MGP pays dividends to its Class A shareholders and consolidated subsidiaries, indicating a commitment to returning value to its investors.

The relationships outlined involve the concepts of Currency Translation Adjustment and Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment, both of which are integral components of Other Comprehensive Income. 

1. **Currency Translation Adjustment** is categorized under **Other Comprehensive Income**, indicating that it reflects the effects of currency changes on a company's financial statements. This adjustment captures the impact of fluctuations in currency values on the overall financial performance.

2. **Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment** is also classified as a part of **Other Comprehensive Income**, similarly reflecting the effects of currency fluctuations on a company's financial results. This adjustment serves to account for the variations in exchange rates that can affect the valuation of foreign assets and liabilities.

In summary, both Currency Translation Adjustment and Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment are essential elements of Other Comprehensive Income, highlighting how currency fluctuations influence a company's financial reporting and overall economic standing.

2. **Trademarks and Foreign Countries**: Conversely, trademarks may face infringement in foreign countries where legal protections are weaker or less comprehensive than those in the United States. This suggests a vulnerability for trademark holders when operating internationally, as the lack of stringent laws in other jurisdictions can lead to unauthorized use or exploitation of their trademarks.

In summary, the relationships underscore the importance of U.S. laws in protecting trademarks while also highlighting the risks associated with international trademark infringement due to varying legal standards in foreign countries.

### Summary of Relationships

1. **Employer and Employee**:
   - The Employer hires the Employee, establishing a contractual relationship that includes terms of employment, salary, and obligations. The Employer is responsible for compensating the Employee, providing fringe benefits, and reimbursing reasonable business expenses incurred during work. The Employee is expected to cooperate with the Employer regarding work-related matters.

2. **Employer's Rights and Obligations**:
   - The Employer assumes all rights and obligations under the employment agreement with the Employee, which is governed by a formal Agreement that outlines the terms and conditions of employment. The Employer holds rights over any Intellectual Property created by the Employee during their employment.

3. **Contractual and Legal Framework**:
   - The Employer and Employee are bound by a contractual agreement that includes provisions for non-competition and confidentiality. The Employer's successors have the right to enforce these provisions, ensuring continuity of obligations. Specific exceptions to the survival of covenants are outlined in Sections 10.2.4 of the Agreement.

4. **Employer's Authority and Responsibilities**:
   - The Employer has the authority to impose restrictions on the Employee during a Restrictive Period following separation. Additionally, the Employer is responsible for selecting a Physician to assess the Employee's disability and is obligated to pay fees associated with arbitration services for dispute resolution.

5. **Employer's Operational Licenses**:
   - The Employer possesses essential licenses for its operations, which are critical for its business activities and may be at risk.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and responsibilities between the Employer and Employee, emphasizing the contractual nature of their relationship, the rights and obligations involved, and the legal framework that governs their interactions.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the regulatory framework governing institutional investors and their interactions with specific laws and exemptions.

1. **Institutional Investors and Rule 504**: Institutional investors are required to comply with Rule 504 when acquiring equity ownership. This rule specifies the necessary waivers and approvals based on ownership percentages, emphasizing the regulatory obligations that these investors must follow.

2. **Institutional Investors and the Michigan Act**: There is a notable exemption for institutional investors under the Michigan Act. Those holding amounts below certain thresholds are not subject to the suitability requirements of the Act, indicating a differentiated treatment of investors based on their investment size.

3. **Exemption and Confidential Information**: Section 8.3 provides guidelines regarding the disclosure of Confidential Information. It specifies that the provisions concerning confidentiality do not apply when disclosure is mandated by law or legal authorities, thus outlining the conditions under which sensitive information may be shared.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the complex regulatory landscape that institutional investors navigate, including specific rules they must follow and exemptions that may apply based on their investment levels and the nature of the information they handle.
